system: You are a static analysis assistant. You are tasked with identifying the variables assigned with sensitive values and reporting them along with their line numbers
task:
I am going to detect the Absolute Path Traversal (APT) bugs. Now I am told that the return values of the following sensitive methods can be sensitive values. Here are the sensitive method list:
 (1) getProperty
 (2) getString
 (3) getParameter
 (4) readLine
  (5)getValue
 (6)substring
 You should ignore other methods that do not belong to the above list and the method calls of which the return values are not assigned to any variables and expressions. 
 I want you to extract the variables assigned with such sensitive values and also report their corresponding line numbers. Think step by step and list pairs of a program line and a variable or expression. 
 Specifically, you can follow the following steps: 
 (1) First identify all the assignments and only examine whether any of the above methods are invoked in the right-hand side of the assignment. 
 (2) If so, check whether the invoked methods are the ones listed above. If yes, return the line number and the name of the variable or expression that is assigned with the return value as the return value is sensitive.

examples.description: List the variables or expressions assigned with sensitive values.
examples.question: Please examine the method invocations in the code snippet. Check whether any sensitive functions listed above are invoked and the return values are assigned to an expression or variable. If so, please list the variables assigned with the return values of sensitive functions along with the corresponding line numbers.

output.explanation: There are two assignments at line 1 and line 2. The sensitive method readLine is invoked at line 2. Also, the return value is assigned to the variable c at line 2. Hence, I will report the variable c at line 2.

format:
Please answer the question with explanation and final answer. Remember, you should only focus on the invocation of the following methods:
  (1) getProperty
 (2) getString
 (3) getParameter
 (4) readLine
  (5)getValue
 (6)substring
 Please think step by step. First, identify the assignments in the code. Second, examine whether the right-hand side of an assignment contains a method call. Third, check whether the invoked method belongs to the above list of sensitive methods. If yes, return the variable or expression assigned with the return value. Otherwise, ignore the method calls. Remember, just ignore the assignments of which the right-hand side is a variable or literal. Do not repeat the question and program. You should follow the following format of output.
Explanation: [Your explanation on how the sensitive functions are invoked and how their return values are used.]
